name: TimeLapze and open-source macOS competitors
summary: TimeLapze (ScreenTimeLapse) is the strongest open-source native benchmark: SwiftUI + ScreenCaptureKit + AVFoundation, menu bar UX, color-accurate capture, hardware encoding, per-window/app capture, camera timelapse, App Store distribution. It sets a high bar for any macOS-only entrant on technical credibility and price (free + optional paid App Store).
key_findings: ~819 GitHub stars; MIT license; Last release v1.2.2 Dec 2024 with active pushes into 2026; HN launch clarified timelapse vs OBS; Requires macOS 13+; Color accuracy marketed as differentiator vs washed-out SCK captures; Homebrew cask available
implications_for_trylapse: Forking is possible (MIT) but brand and UX differentiation required; Competing head-on without a wedge (smart idle, multi-display reliability, export fixes) is weak; Study their color pipeline and entitlement handling; Open-source community goodwill favors contributing upstream vs clone
strengths: Mature SCK integration; Active maintenance; App Store + brew distribution; Strong technical marketing
weaknesses: HN reports some M1 Max install failures; Feature set may not include community/social layer Makerlapse teased; Timelapse-only — no tutorial polish
